application_summary.application_purpose: Provide a simple command-line greeting flow that outputs a personalized or default greeting.
key_functionalities: Build greeting text from template and recipient
Use default recipient when no usable input is provided
application_summary.architecture_pattern: Layered mini-flow (controller -> service -> repository) with constructor-based dependency wiring

responsibilities: Behavior verification for output and greeting rules

files.summary: Legacy/simple entrypoint that prints a fixed greeting.
files.description: This file always outputs a static greeting text. It represents a fixed greeting behavior without recipient personalization. It serves the basic greeting use case in its simplest form.
business_rules: Printed greeting text is always 'Hello World'.

method_breakdown.significance: Implements the most basic greeting behavior.

files.summary: Supplies canonical greeting template and default recipient values.
files.description: This file defines the standard phrase structure and fallback recipient used by the greeting flow. It centralizes default wording choices that shape user-facing output. It provides reusable domain defaults for consistent greetings.

method_breakdown.significance: Controls consistent phrasing across all greetings.
Defines the no-input fallback value used in greeting logic.

files.summary: Builds final greeting text based on recipient input and defaults.
files.description: This file applies recipient selection rules and creates the final greeting string. It ensures blank or missing recipient input is replaced with a default recipient. It normalizes non-blank input before composing output.
business_rules: Service dependency must be non-null at construction.
If recipient input is null or blank, use default recipient.
If recipient input is present, trim whitespace before use.
Final greeting is template formatted with selected recipient.

method_breakdown.summary: Creates service with required repository dependency.
parameters: greetingRepository (GreetingRepository): source of template and defaults
method_breakdown.significance: Enforces availability of greeting defaults and template source.

method_breakdown.summary: Generates greeting from requested recipient or fallback.
method_breakdown.significance: Core business logic for personalized vs default greeting behavior.

files.summary: Selects recipient from CLI arguments and delegates greeting creation.
files.description: This file decides whether to use caller input or default path. It maps command-line arguments to greeting requests and forwards them to service logic. It encapsulates simple request-routing rules for greeting generation.
business_rules: Controller dependency must be non-null at construction.
If args are null or empty, request default-recipient greeting.
If args are present, only first argument is used as recipient.

method_breakdown.summary: Creates controller with required greeting service.
method_breakdown.significance: Guarantees controller can execute greeting flow.

method_breakdown.summary: Converts args into recipient choice and returns greeting.
method_breakdown.significance: Entry rule point for selecting default or explicit recipient.

files.summary: Application entrypoint that wires components and prints greeting output.
files.description: This file runs the greeting use case from startup to user-visible output. It assembles greeting components and invokes the controller path. It realizes the end-to-end greeting workflow for command-line usage.
business_rules: Application prints exactly one greeting generated from controller logic.

method_breakdown.significance: Operational entrypoint for the full greeting flow.

files.summary: Defines expected greeting behaviors for output, argument handling, and fallback logic.
files.description: This file captures acceptance expectations for the greeting workflow. It verifies default greeting output, first-argument recipient behavior, and blank-input fallback handling. It provides executable evidence of business rules.
business_rules: Main flow output must be 'Hello World' followed by system newline.
Controller must use first argument as recipient.
Blank recipient input must fall back to 'World'.

method_breakdown.summary: Validates default output of main including newline.

method_breakdown.significance: Confirms default-recipient business rule under empty input.

configuration_analysis.business_impact: Ensures Java 25 compilation and automated validation of greeting rules via tests.
